commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=ea1e5c82c49ddbbe3461841242e90adb207cd013)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a read-tool-local buffer decoder with BOM, strict UTF-8, and GBK fallback behavior, wires it into the session read tool, and adds read-tool tests for GBK and BOM cases.

PR surface: Source +133, Tests +72. Total +205 across 3 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.6 decode session read-tool text buffers as UTF-8, and a Node probe shows the linked GBK sample rejects strict UTF-8 but decodes as Chinese with GBK. I did not run a live Chinese Windows OpenClaw session in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer selection of a canonical codepage-aware branch and contributor-supplied real behavior proof, not a safe autonomous repair on this branch.

target.title: fix(tools): auto-detect file encoding in read tool for GBK support
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #92664.

The read tool always decoded files as UTF-8, producing garbled text when reading GBK-encoded files common on Chinese Windows.

## What changed

- Added `decodeFileBuffer()` in `src/agents/sessions/tools/encoding.ts` with encoding auto-detection: BOM check → strict UTF-8 → GBK fallback → lenient UTF-8.
- Updated `read.ts` to use `decodeFileBuffer(buffer)` instead of `buffer.toString("utf-8")`.
- Updated `Buffer.byteLength` call to use encoding-aware length calculation.
- No schema change required — encoding detection is transparent.

## Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed:** Read tool displays garbled text when reading GBK-encoded files.

**Real environment tested:** Node v22.19.0, Linux x64, commit ea1e5c8.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/sessions/tools/read.test.ts

**Evidence after fix (terminal capture):**

$ node -e "const {decodeFileBuffer} = require(\"./src/agents/sessions/tools/encoding.js\"); const gbk = Buffer.from([0xce,0xc4,0xbc,0xfe,0x0a,0x73,0x65,0x63,0x6f,0x6e,0x64]); console.log(\"GBK decode:\", JSON.stringify(decodeFileBuffer(gbk)));"

GBK decode: "文件\nsecond"

$ node -e "const {decodeFileBuffer} = require(\"./src/agents/sessions/tools/encoding.js\"); const utf8 = Buffer.from(\"hello world\"); console.log(\"UTF-8 decode:\", JSON.stringify(decodeFileBuffer(utf8)));"

UTF-8 decode: "hello world"

All 11 read tool tests pass including 6 new encoding detection tests:
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  11 passed (11)
   Duration  2.50s

exit code: 0

**Observed result after fix:** GBK-encoded Chinese text (\xce\xc4\xbc\xfe → 文件) correctly decoded. UTF-8 files continue to work without regression. The decodeFileBuffer function correctly detects BOM signatures, validates strict UTF-8, and falls back to GBK for legacy Windows encodings.

**What was not tested:** Big5/Shift_JIS encodings beyond the GBK fallback. Actual Windows console codepage (CP936) end-to-end.
target.author: chengzhichao-xydt
